market_context: Stock futures are climbing following reports of a U.S. 15-point plan to end hostilities with Iran, delivered via Pakistan, boosting sentiment after Tuesday's decline due to Middle East unrest and higher oil prices[1]. S&P 500 E-Mini futures (ESM26) are up +0.87% at 6,663.50, with pre-market gains in tech leaders like NVDA (+1.39%), TSLA (+1.93%), and AAPL (+0.81%)[5]. Nikkei rebound faces Iran risks, while U.S. PMI data remains in focus amid waning de-escalation optimism[2][5].

predictions.reasoning: S&P 500 E-Mini futures (ESM26) are up +0.87% at 6,663.50 amid positive reaction to U.S.-Iran de-escalation plan, reversing Tuesday's oil-driven decline[1][5]. Pre-market momentum in key S&P components like NVDA and AAPL supports broader index gains[5]. Robinhood futures markets show strong probability above 6525-6600 levels[4].

Nasdaq futures gain momentum with pre-market strength in Nasdaq-100 leaders: NVDA +1.39%, TSLA +1.93%, GOOGL +1.17%, AAPL +0.81%[5]. Geopolitical de-escalation news favors risk-on tech rally after recent Middle East concerns[1][5]. $IUXX at 24,002.45 reflects positive setup despite minor -0.77% dip[5].

Dow futures rise on U.S.-Iran peace plan, with pre-market gains in industrials like TRI +1.82%, UAL +2.17%, and BLK +0.60%[1][5]. Tuesday's +1.38% Dow close sets positive base despite oil pressures[6]. Broader futures uptrend supports end-of-day gains[3][5].

TSLA leads pre-market movers at +1.93% to 390.43, benefiting from Nasdaq momentum and reduced geopolitical risk[5]. Tech sector rotation amid de-escalation news favors high-beta names like TSLA[1][5]. Recent volatility patterns show upside potential on positive catalysts[5].
